preamble: Backing of the velar fricative [x] to the uvular fricative [χ] occurs in several languages, often as a dialectal feature or triggered by back vowels.

examples.note: The 'hard G' in Northern Dutch is frequently realized as a uvular fricative [χ] rather than a velar [x].
In many German dialects, the 'ach-laut' is backed to a uvular [χ] when following low back vowels like [a].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
